title: EPA v. UTICA (MUN), CITY OF
text:
EPA Enforcement Case 02-1977-0005: UTICA (MUN), CITY OF
Type: Civil Judicial Action
Status: Closed
Outcome: Final Order With Penalty
Lead Agency: EPA

Case Summary:
CITY WAS SUED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH ITS NPDES PERMIT  GOVERNING DISCHARGES FROM ITS SEWER SYSTEM AND TWO SUB-      SEQUENT ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS.  THE NPDES VIOLATIONS,        CONTINUOUS FROM 7/1/75, RESULTED FROM NUMEROUS DRY WEATHER   OVERFLOWS OF RAW SEWAGE TO THE MOHAWK RIVER.  THE COMPLAINT  SOUGHT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND A $1,000,000 CIVIL PENALTY.     SEE STATUS COMMENTS FOR LATER DEVELOPMENTS.

Penalties and Costs:
Federal Penalty: $5,000

Laws and Sections Violated:
  CWA: 309: Violation of Existing AO, 402: Clean Water Act, 301: NPDES Discharge without a Permit (Administrative Compliance Order, Other Unpermitted Discharges)

Defendants:
  NEW YORK, STATE OF (3RD PARTY DEF)
  ONEIDA COUNTY SEWER DISTRICT (3RD PARTY)
  ONEIDA COUNTY, NY (3RD PARTY DEF)
  UTICA, NY, CITY OF

Facilities:
  ONEIDA COUNTY WATER POLLUTION CONTROL PLANT, 51 LELAND AVE., UTICA, NY

Case Milestones:
  03/09/1977: Referred To Headquarters
  05/26/1977: Referred TO U.S. Attorney
  05/26/1977: Referred To Dept Of Justice
  06/02/1977: Complaint Filed With Court
  08/16/1977: Final Order Lodged
  08/16/1977: Concluded
  08/16/1977: Final Order Entered
  10/10/1991: Enforcement Action Closed

Pollutants:
  NONE

Enforcement Conclusion #1: Consent Decree or Court Order Resolving a Civil Judicial Action
  Name: UTICA, CITY OF
  Settlement Entered: 08/16/1977
  Federal Penalty: $5,000

Violations:
Violation Of A Permit Requirement
